main_observation: In this exploratory top 5 confidence group, happy was the most frequent dominant emotion label(s), appeared alongside 3/5 recordings, and had a mean dominant-emotion probability of 0.92.
In this exploratory bottom 5 confidence group, calm and happy were the most frequent dominant emotion label(s), appeared alongside 2/5 recordings, and had a mean dominant-emotion probability of 0.58.
In this exploratory top 5 clarity group, happy was the most frequent dominant emotion label(s), appeared alongside 3/5 recordings, and had a mean dominant-emotion probability of 0.88.
In this exploratory bottom 5 clarity group, happy was the most frequent dominant emotion label(s), appeared alongside 2/5 recordings, and had a mean dominant-emotion probability of 0.63.
In this exploratory top 5 engagement group, happy was the most frequent dominant emotion label(s), appeared alongside 3/5 recordings, and had a mean dominant-emotion probability of 0.92.
In this exploratory bottom 5 engagement group, happy and calm were the most frequent dominant emotion label(s), appeared alongside 2/5 recordings, and had a mean dominant-emotion probability of 0.75.
